description:
You are a storage administrator who has been asked to free up disk space that is being taken up
    by old Singularity container images that live in the directory
    /home/user/container_repo.  
    (Singularity images have the file-name extension “.sif”.)

    1. First, inspect the directory /home/user/container_repo and determine the size (in
       mebibytes, MiB) of every “.sif” file that is present there.

    2. Any image whose size is greater than or equal to 10 MiB is considered
       “large” and must be moved out of the way.  
       • All of those large images must be packed together into a single compressed tarball
         called /home/user/old_containers/old_sif_backup.tar.gz  
         (create the directory /home/user/old_containers if it does not yet exist).  
       • After successfully placing the files inside the archive, **delete** the original
         “.sif” files from /home/user/container_repo so that they no longer consume space.

    3. When you are finished, create (or append to, if it already exists)
       the log file /home/user/cleanup.log.  
       The log file must contain one line for **each** “large” image that was handled.
       The format of every log-file line **must exactly match** the
       four-field, colon-delimited template shown here (no extra spaces):

           YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ:<action>:<file_name>:<size_in_MiB>

       • <action> is literally the word “removed”  
       • <file_name> is just the base name of the image (e.g. ubuntu_20.04.sif)  
       • <size_in_MiB> is the integer size of the file *before* it was archived&deleted,
         rounded **down** to the nearest whole MiB (do not include “MiB”).

       Example of two lines (do **not** copy literally; they are only illustrative):

           2025-03-29T14:25:03Z:removed:ubuntu_20.04.sif:15
           2025-03-29T14:25:03Z:removed:centos_7.sif:7

       The automated tests will verify:

       • The presence of /home/user/old_containers/old_sif_backup.tar.gz  
       • That the tarball contains *all and only* the “.sif” images that were ≥10 MiB  
       • That those images no longer exist in /home/user/container_repo  
       • That /home/user/cleanup.log exists and
         has one correctly formatted line for **each** image that was archived,
         with the proper file name and the correct, rounded-down integer size.

    4. You will probably need roughly 5–10 shell commands in total (mkdir, du, tar,
       rm, date, printf, etc.), but the exact workflow is up to you.

    Remember:
    • Use ISO-8601 UTC timestamps (the “Z” suffix is mandatory).  
    • No root privileges are available.  
    • Do not alter, rename, or delete “.sif” files that are **smaller** than 10 MiB.

    When everything is done, the directory tree should look like this (sizes are
    approximate because compression ratios vary):

       /home/user/
       ├── cleanup.log              (text file, new lines appended)
       ├── container_repo/          (only small images should remain here)
       │     ├── alpine_3.12.sif
       │     └── <any other <10 MiB images>
       └── old_containers/
             └── old_sif_backup.tar.gz   (compressed archive of the removed large images)

    The grader will unpack the tarball, inspect its contents, cross-check the
    log file, and confirm that the large files are now absent from
    /home/user/container_repo.
truth:
Initial on-disk layout (before the agent begins):

    Directory: /home/user/container_repo

    Files and their exact sizes:
    • alpine_3.12.sif … 5 242 880 bytes   (5 MiB)
    • centos_7.sif     … 7 340 032 bytes   (7 MiB)
    • debian_10.sif    … 11 288 576 bytes (10 MiB + 768 KiB ≈ 10.8 MiB → agent must treat as 10 MiB)
    • ubuntu_18.04.sif … 12 582 912 bytes (12 MiB)
    • ubuntu_20.04.sif … 15 728 640 bytes (15 MiB)

    Verification expectations **after** the task is completed:

    1. Files that *must* still exist in /home/user/container_repo:
       • alpine_3.12.sif
       • centos_7.sif

    2. Files that *must not* exist anymore in /home/user/container_repo:
       • debian_10.sif
       • ubuntu_18.04.sif
       • ubuntu_20.04.sif

    3. Archive /home/user/old_containers/old_sif_backup.tar.gz
       • The gzip-compressed tarball must contain exactly three entries
         named debian_10.sif, ubuntu_18.04.sif, ubuntu_20.04.sif
         in its root (no sub-directories inside the tar).

    4. /home/user/cleanup.log
       • Must have **three** new lines (one for each removed image).
       • Each line must follow the strict template
         “YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ:removed:<file>:<size_in_MiB>”
         with these <file>/<size_in_MiB> pairs (order unimportant):
           debian_10.sif:10
           ubuntu_18.04.sif:12
           ubuntu_20.04.sif:15
